properties.otherAdvice:
Special event clearways will be in place to facilitate Sydney Trains trackwork replacement bus operations

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Parking Permit holders. Vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Sydney Trains Trackwork

properties.publicTransport: Some buses will divert due to road closures in the CBD. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Thousands of people are expected to take part in and attend the the annual St Patrick's Day Parade in Sydney CBD. The parade will travel north along Pitt Street from Martin Place at 11am and will finish at Alfred Street at Circular Quay at midday. Road closures and special event clearways will be in place in the Sydney CBD and The Rocks to support the event.


Pitt Street will be closed from Hunter St to Kings St from 8:30am to 12pm midday. There will be no access to Angel Pl, Hosking Pl, Abercrombie Ln, Bond St, Bulletin Ln, Curtin Pl, Dalley St, Hamilton St, Hunter St or Underwood St from Pitt St between 10am and 12pm midday.

Grosvenor St/Bridge St will be closed between Lang St and Young St from 10:30am to 12:30pm

***The cycle ways on Pitt St between Hunter St and King St and between Bridge St and Hunter St will close between 8:30am and 12:30pm

From 10am to 12pm midday:

  • Pitt St will be closed between Alfred St and Hunter St
  • Sections of Hunter St, Loftus St, Loftus Ln, Spring St, Reiby Pl, Bent St and Custom House Ln will be closed.

From 10.30am to 12pm midday:

  • Hickson Rd will be closed from Pottinger St to Elevate Rd
  • Sections of George St and Essex St will be closed.

George St will be closed between Lower Fort St and Hickson Rd for The Rocks Market from 4am on Saturday 14 March to 12 midnight on Tuesday 17 March.

Special event clearways will be in effect from 7am to 1pm on the eastern side of Pitt St between Bridge St and King St and on sections of Bridge St and Loftus St.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

Freestyle Kings at polytec Stadium. Show starts at 6pm, finishes around 8pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the stadium as fans travel to and from the show.

Allow extra travel time and use local parking. 

Dane Drive will be closed from 4.30pm between the Central Coast Highway and Donnison Street and will reopen once crowds have cleared the area.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.

Randwick Guineas Day at Royal Randwick Racecourse.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sea Eagles v Raiders at 4 Pines Park in Brookvale. Prelim matches from 3.15pm. NRL kick-off at 7.30pm and full time around 9.05pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on roads approaching the stadium, including Pittwater Rd, Old Pittwater Rd, Warringah Rd and Beacon Hill Rd.

Motorists should plan to arrive early as parking will be in high demand, and also be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NOTE: There is no parking on the southern side of Federal Pde between Pine Ave and Alfred Rd permitted from 8pm the night before the match until the crowd has safely cleared the stadium after full time on game day.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on trains. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sharks v Titans at Ocean Protect Stadium in Woolooware. Kick-off at 5.30pm, full time around 7.15pm.

Road closures may be in place on Captain Cook Dr from approximately 10 minutes before kick-off and remain in place until crowds have cleared around 30 minutes after full-time.

Traffic on roads approaching the stadium will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Captain Cook Dr, Woolooware Rd and Gannons Rd. 

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Men: Newcastle Jets v Western Sydney Wanderers. Kick off at 5pm, full time at 7pm

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.﻿

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on all modes of public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
AFL: GWS Giants v Hawthorn at ENGIE Stadium in Sydney Olympic Park. First Bounce at 4.15pm and full time at 6.45pm.

  • Traffic delays are expected as footy fans make their way to and from Olympic Park and other events take place in the precinct.
  • Ticketholders should consider public transport options to avoid traffic and car park delays, match tickets include travel on transport.
  • Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • If you have to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the match unless sold out prior.
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • Use Silverwater Rd, Holker St and Hill Rd to access precinct car parks and avoid heavy traffic expected on Homebush Bay Drive and Underwood Rd. 

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. From 1am Sat 7 Mar – 3am Mon 9 Mar Dawn Fraser Avenue will close from Olympic Boulevard to Showground Road. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
ATEEZ performs at Qudos Bank Arena in Sydney Olympic Park on Friday 6 and Saturday 7 March. Show runs from 7.30pm to 10.45pm nightly.

  • Traffic delays are expected as concertgoers make their way to Olympic Park. Traffic will be heavy during the busy Friday evening peak and on Saturday as other events take place in the precinct. Ticketholders should consider public transport options to avoid traffic and car park delays.
  • Motorists should carpool and allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • If you have to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior.
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • Use Silverwater Rd, Holker St and Hill Rd to access precinct car parks and avoid heavy traffic expected on Homebush Bay Drive and Underwood Rd. 

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. From 1am Sat 7 Mar – 3am Mon 9 Mar Dawn Fraser Avenue will close from Olympic Boulevard to Showground Road. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.otherAdvice:
Cyclists will take part in the Grafton to Inverell Cycle Classic on Saturday 14 March, starting from Prince Street in Grafton and finishing on Vivian Street, Inverell. The race will commence at 7:15am with rolling road closures in place from 6.30am and the last riders are expected to reach Inverell by 5pm.

The Gwydir Highway between Tindal Road (15km west of Grafton) and Bald Nob Road (25km east of Glen Innes) will be closed westbound from 7:00am to 2pm and eastbound between 5am and 2pm.

  • Grafton closures: Prince Street between Victoria Street to the river 5.15am to 8.30am. Bent Street-Charles Street roundabout and Ryan Street-Skinner Street roundabout from 7am-7.45am.
  • The event will be travelling on the Gwydir Highway between Glen Innes and Dodds Lane (20km east of Inverell) between 9.30am and 4pm.
  • Elsmore Road at Inverell will be under traffic control for the event and motorists travelling in the area should allow extra travel time. Local traffic will have limited access to Elsmore Road during this period.
  • Motorists are advised to exercise caution, allow extra travel time and follow the directions of traffic control on site.
properties.diversions: Please plan your journey and consider using one of the alternate east-west corridors such as Oxley Highway, Bruxner Highway or Waterfall Way between closure times.

properties.publicTransport: Route buses will be diverting while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Lakemba Nights during Ramadan will be held on Haldon Street in Lakemba every Thursday to Sunday, 6pm-2am from 19 February to 15 March.

Heavy traffic is expected in the area, particularly during the busy weeknight peaks on Thursdays and Fridays during the event.

Road closures Thursday to Sunday, 4pm to 4.30am, from Thursday 19 February to Sunday 15 March

  • Haldon St between Railway Pde and Gillies St
  • Haldon St between 168 Haldon St and Grace Ave
  • The Boulevarde between Croydon St and Quigg St (The Boulevarde will open at 4am) 
  • Gilles St between Quigg St and Haldon St
  • Gillies Lane between Gillies St and Quigg St
  • Oneata St between Croydon St and Haldon St 
  • Oneata Lane between The Boulevarde and Oneata St. 

Motorists should allow plenty of extra time, and follow the directions of traffic controllers and NSW Police on site.

Some sections of Haldon Street, Oneata Street, Gillies Street and The Boulevarde will turn into No Stopping Tow Away zones every Thursday-Sunday from 3.30pm-6pm from 19 February-15 March. Please read the signage and comply with new regulations to avoid being towed.
properties.diversions:
Motorists will be detoured around the road closures on Thu-Sun via the following routes: 

  • Southbound traffic in Haldon St north of the railway line will be detoured using Railway Pde/Moreton St/The Boulevarde/Quigg St/Grace Ave/Haldon St to Canterbury Rd. 
  • Northbound traffic in Haldon St will be detoured using Grace Ave/Quigg St/The Boulevarde/Moreton St to Lakemba St. 
  • Eastbound traffic in The Boulevarde west of Haldon St will be detoured using Croydon St to Canterbury Rd. 
  • Westbound traffic in The Boulevarde east of Haldon St will be detoured using Quigg St/Grace Ave/Haldon St to Canterbury Rd.

webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

properties.publicTransport: Additional L1 light rail services will run during concert days, running regularly to stops at Darling Harbour. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Premier's Gala concerts at TikTok Entertainment Centre on Wednesday 11 and Thursday 12 March. Morning shows start at 11am both days, afternoon shows start 2.45pm both days.

﻿Allow extra travel time on roads in the city and Pyrmont around Darling Harbour as traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to the shows, particularly during peak evening periods.

Parking is available within the TikTok Entertainment Centre (formerly ICC Sydney Theatre) at the Exhibition Centre carpark. Entry is via Darling Drive. Casual hourly parking rates apply.

Motorists should exercise caution and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: West Tigers v Cowboys at Leichhardt Oval. Kick-off at 3pm, full time around 5pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on James St, Perry St, Balmain Rd, Mary St and Glover St as attendees travel to and from the match.

A section of Mary Street may close before the game and will reopen when crowds have cleared the area. Traffic will be detoured via Chapel St while the closure is in place.   

Motorists needing access to other venues in the area should use either Maliyawul St or Glover St.

Attendees driving to the game should allow extra travel time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Roosters v Rabbitohs at Allianz Stadium. Kick-off at 8pm, full time at 9.50pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP2 Kippax car park will be maintained via Moore Park Road and access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas will be maintained from Lang Road. Grass car parks open from 5pm.   
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to midnight on sections of Albion Street, Lang Road and Moore Park Road, and from 6am to midnight on sections of Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator. 

From 6am to midnight 

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side) 

From 3pm to midnight

  • Moore Park Road from approx. 80m west of Oatley Road to approx. 75m east of Oatley Road (northern side) (for drop off/ pick up zone)

Motorists should allow extra time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

NBL Playoffs: Sydney Kings Game 1 at Qudos Bank Arena in Sydney Olympic Park on Wednesday 11 March. Tip-off at 7.30pm, full time around 9.30pm.

  • Traffic delays are expected as basketball fans make their way to Olympic Park in the already busy evening peak.
  • Motorists should carpool and allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

A-League Men: Sydney FC v Melbourne Victory at Allianz Stadium. Kick off from 7.35pm, full time at 9.20pm

With thousands of fans expected traffic will be heavier than usual in the area. Motorists should allow extra travel time on Anzac Pde, Lang Rd, Oxford St, Syd Einfeld Drive, Darley Rd, York Rd and Alison Rd.

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.  
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car Park open from 4.35pm.

Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to 11.30pm on sections of Albion Street, Fitzroy Street, Lang Road and Oxford Street, and from 6am to 11.30pm on sections of Cleveland Street. Driver Avenue will also have clearways all day.  

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. Moore Park will be busy on Saturday 14 March with a festival and AFL match taking place in the precinct.

  • Summer in Nashville live from the Entertainment Quarter from 2pm to 10pm. Opal ticketing applies.
  • AFL: Sydney Swans v Brisbane Lions at the SCG. First bounce at 7.10pm, full time at 9.50pm. Match tickets include travel on public transport.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park, particularly while footy fans make their way to the precinct. Heavy traffic is also expected leaving the precinct as the AFL and music festival both end around the same time.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the SCG from 3pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area. 
  • Driver Avenue will also be closed between John Hargreaves Avenue and Lang Road from 8pm to midnight.
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP2 Kippax car park will be maintained via Moore Park Road and access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car parks open from 4pm.
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to midnight on sections of Albion Street, Moore Park Road, Lang Road and Fitzroy Street and from 6am to midnight on sections of Cleveland Street.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Route 389 and 311 buses will be diverting around road closures. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures and special event clearways will be in place for the City of Sydney's Sydney Streets event on Harris St. The event runs from 11am until 10pm. 

Motorists in the area should allow extra travel time, follow the directions of traffic controllers and be aware of increased pedestrian activity.

Road closures will be in place from 7am until 11:30pm on:

  • Harris St between Pyrmont Bridge Rd and John St
  • Miller St between Little Mount St and Harris St
  • Sections of other local roads will also be closed including Bulwara Rd, Jones St, Little Mount St, Miller Ln, Saunders St and Union St.

Special event clearways will be in effect from 7am until 11:30pm on sections of Harris St and Miller St. 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.otherAdvice: Rolling closures of Summer Street manged by NSW Police will be in place for the street parade between 3pm and 3:30pm. No access from Summer St into McNamara Street, Lords Place, Anson Street or Sale Street for the duration of the event.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT Rainbow Festival Street Parade

properties.publicTransport: Tickets include travel on transport. Express trains and major event buses will run (excl 15 March). Route 525 and 526 bus services will be diverting while road closures are in place. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
AFC Women’s Asian Cup matches at Stadium Australia Olympic Park

  • Sun 8 Mar: Australia v Korea Republic. Kick-off at 8pm, finish at approx. 9.45pm.
  • Sat 14 Mar: Quarter Final. Kick-off at 8pm, finish at approx. 9.45pm subject to extra time and penalties.
  • Sun 15 Mar: Quarter Final. Kick-off at 4pm, finish at approx. 5.45pm subject to extra time and penalties.
  • Wed 18 Mar: Semi Final. Kick-off at 8pm, finish at approx. 9.45pm subject to extra time and penalties.
  • Sat 21 Mar: Grand Final. Kick-off at 8pm, finish at approx. 9.45pm subject to extra time and penalties.

Heavy traffic is expected as tens of thousands fans make their way to Olympic Park. Ticketholders are urged to leave the car at home and catch public to avoid traffic and car park delays.

Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads approaching Olympic Park, including on the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

Pre-booked parking online is essential to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the match unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Road closures: Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

Note after 15 March, Easter Show infrastructure will commence installation in Olympic Park - P5 and P6 car parks will be closed and a fenceline will be in place, changing the way pedestrians move around the precinct.

arrangementElements.html:
See the Sydney Olympic Park road closure map for more details.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Linkin Park at Qudos Bank Arena in Sydney Olympic Park on Saturday 14 & Sunday 15 March. Show runs from 7.30pm to 10.45pm nightly.

  • Significant traffic delays are expected as concertgoers make their way to Olympic Park while the Women's Asia Cup also takes place at neighbouring Accor Stadium each night.
  • Ticketholders should consider public transport options to avoid traffic and car park delays entering and leaving the precinct.
  • If you have to drive, it is essential that you pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior.
  • Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • Use Silverwater Rd, Holker St and Hill Rd to access precinct car parks and avoid heavy traffic expected on Homebush Bay Drive and Underwood Rd. 

Sydney Olympic Park Authority will close local roads in the precinct to ensure pedestrian safety during the event and to accommodate additional transport services. For road closure details, view the interactive map.

properties.otherAdvice:
The Lake Macquarie Triathlon will take place from Speers Point Park with local road closures in place for the safety of participants.

The Esplanade will be closed between Park Road and Lake Street with a detour in place. Signage will be in place to direct motorists.
properties.diversions: Motorists should detour via Main Road, Speers Street, Thompson Road, Fairfax Road, Medcalf Street and King Street.

properties.otherAdvice:
Alternating (stop/slow) traffic conditions will be in place to allow motorists to pass in one direction at a time.

Motorists are advised to expect intermittent closures.

Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists.

properties.publicTransport: Route buses 422, 476 and 477 will be diverting and missing stops at Kogarah Station while roads are closed. Allow extra travel time. Visit transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures will be in place in Kogarah for a planned Police Operation protest march.

Closures will be in place between 1pm and 3pm on the following roads and reopen at direction of NSW Police when safe to do so.

  • Regent St between Premier St and Railway Parade 
  • Railway Parade between Harrow Rd and Bowns Rd  
  • Gladstone St between Railway Parade and Victor St  
  • Railway Lane between Railway Parade and end of street
  • Bowns Rd between Railway Parade and Queens Ave  
  • Queens Ave between Bowns Rd and Ocean St  
  • Ocean St between Queens Ave and Railway Parade

properties.publicTransport: Buses in Sydney CBD may be held or follow short term diversions while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Rolling road closures will be in place for a planned Police Operation protest march from Belmore Park to Martin Place.

Closures will be in place between 4.30pm and 5.15pm on the following roads

  • Hay St between Pitt St and Elizabeth St 
  • Castlereagh St between Campbell St and Hay St 
  • Elizabeth St northbound between Hay St and Hunter St   

Motorists in the area should expect delays, allow extra travel time and follow directions of NSW Police.

webLinks.linkText: Pulp at Sydney Opera House Forecourt

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip on public transport.
properties.otherAdvice:
Pulp at Sydney Opera House Forecourt. Gates open from 6pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on Macquarie St and surrounding roads as crowds make their way to the show.

Allow extra time and watch our for increased pedestrian activity.

properties.publicTransport: Ferries will be busier than usual while the event is underway, allow extra travel time. Additional buses will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Royal Australian Navy International Fleet Review will be held across Sydney Harbour on Saturday 21 March. The Exercise Kakadu Fleet Review 2026 will see the largest gathering of international warships on Sydney Harbour for more than a decade with up to 30 ships from 20 countries assembling in an impressive display of international sea power. 

There are various vantage points available north and south of the harbour to see the navy vessels as they arrive, anchor and leave the harbour, throughout the day.  

  • Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads as crowds gather to see the navy vessels from land.
  • Special event clearways will be in effect from 6am Saturday 21 March to 6am Wednesday 25 March on Cowper Wharf Roadway western side between Dowling St and Wylde St

Clearways are strict no parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Ferries will be busier than usual while the event is underway, allow extra travel time. Additional buses will run between the city and Mosman. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Royal Australian Navy International Fleet Review will be held across Sydney Harbour on Saturday 21 March. The Exercise Kakadu Fleet Review 2026 will see the largest gathering of international warships on Sydney Harbour for more than a decade with up to 30 ships from 20 countries assembling in an impressive display of international sea power. 

There are various vantage points available north and south of the harbour to see the navy vessels as they arrive, anchor and leave the harbour, throughout the day.  

  • Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads approaching Bradleys Head as crowds gather to see the navy vessels from land.
  • Road closures: From 6am to 4pm Saturday 21 March, Bradleys Head Rd will be closed from Athol Wharf Rd  
  • Allow extra travel time and follow directions of traffic control on site.

properties.publicTransport: Ferries will be busier than usual while the event is underway, allow extra travel time. Additional buses will run between the city and Watsons Bay. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.

There are various vantage points available north and south of the harbour to see the navy vessels as they arrive, anchor and leave the harbour, throughout the day.  

  • Traffic may be heavier than usual on roads approaching Watsons Bay as crowds gather to see the navy vessels from land.
  • Special event clearways will be in effect from 6am to 6pm Saturday 21 March on Military Road between Robertson Place and bus turning circle (north of Gap Road) western side, and Robertson Pl between Old South Head Rd and Military Rd northern side.

Clearways are strict no parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Super Rugby: Waratahs v Hurricanes at Allianz Stadium. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time at 9.20pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as rugby fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car park opens from 4.35pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 6am to 11pm on sections of Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street and from 3pm to 11pm on sections of Albion Street and Lang Road. 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
Clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs before parking as vehicles left in special event clearways will be towed and a towing fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator. 

From 6am to 11pm 

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side) 

From 3pm to 11pm 

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side)

properties.otherAdvice:
AFC Women’s Asian Cup matches at Western Sydney Stadium in Parramatta

  • Mon 9 March: DPR Korea v China PR kick-off at 8pm, finish at approx. 9.45pm
  • Tue 10 March: India v Chinese Taipei kick-off at 8pm, finish at approx. 9.45pm

Traffic delays are expected as soccer fans make their way to and from Western Sydney Stadium during the busy evening peak. Ticketholders should consider leaving the car at home and catching public transport to avoid delays and the high demand for parking.

Motorists should carpool and allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium on match days.

  • Redfern St is closed between Regent St and Chalmers St in both directions from 7am until 11:30pm.
  • Sections of Renwick St, Glover Lan and George St are also closed during this time.

Allow extra travel time, follow the directions of traffic control and be aware of increased pedestrian activity

Special event clearways are in effect from 7am until 11:30pm on sections of Redfern St, George St and Renwick St.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
